summary: Gruber writes a satirical screenplay-style piece personifying the White iPhone 4 as a washed-up, cocaine-addled celebrity being confronted by the Original iPhone in a Las Vegas bar. The piece dramatizes Apple's repeated delays of the white iPhone 4, which was originally supposed to ship in summer 2010 but kept getting pushed back. The Original iPhone delivers a brutal intervention, pointing out that the White iPhone 4 has never actually shipped to a single customer and has only embarrassed the company. The satire captures the absurdity of a product that generated constant press coverage despite not existing as a purchasable item, and the awkward position Apple found itself in having to repeatedly apologize for delays.
key_points: The white iPhone 4 had been delayed from July to end of year to spring 2011, becoming an embarrassment for Apple
A brief appearance in the Apple Store app generated another round of press coverage for a product that couldn't actually be purchased
Apple was forced into the unusual position of publicly apologizing for disappointing customers
By the time the white iPhone 4 would finally ship in spring, a new iPhone model would be only months away, making it nearly obsolete on arrival
The piece mocks the conspiracy theories that the delay was strategic — for a Verizon launch or antenna fix — when the reality was simply manufacturing problems
money_quotes: She's made actual movies, which have sold actual tickets. She had an actual career to ruin. What have you done, at all, other than embarrass yourself?
You're not the rebel who's fallen down. You're the loser who's never done a damn thing.
Interest in what? A phone that can't be bought?
The truth, plain and obvious, does not compute: you're a complete fuck-up.
You made the Company say that they're sorry for disappointing customers. Think about that.
You get 12 months in the spotlight, kid. That's it. Trust me, I know. And you're set to blow all of them.
The Company might as well have said 'when hell freezes over' instead of 'spring'.
The only iPhone bust. The Great White Nope.
key_insight: The white iPhone 4's repeated delays turned it from a premium product variant into an industry punchline, with its constant press coverage only highlighting the absurdity of a product that generated more headlines than sales.
